channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Lamine Yamal Matches Jay-Jay Okocha’s World Cup Dribbling Record in 2026 Tournament
item.description: Lamine Yamal, the Spanish teenage sensation, has matched a World Cup record set by Nigerian legend Jay-Jay Okocha 28 years ago, highlighting his growing influence at the 2026 FIFA World Cup. According...

item.title: Alexandra Eala advances to Wimbledon quarterfinals after stunning Iga Swiatek
item.description: Rising Filipina tennis star Alexandra Eala made a dramatic statement at Wimbledon by defeating top-seeded Iga Swiatek in a third-round clash. The match, held on Centre Court, saw Eala overcome the wor...

item.title: Ireland secures narrow victory over Wallabies in Nations Championship opener
item.description: In a tightly contested match at Sydney Football Stadium, Ireland edged out Australia 33-31 in the Nations Championship opener. The Wallabies, led by flyhalf Ben Donaldson, struggled to capitalize on l...

item.title: Far-right AfD Party Confronts Protests in Germany as It Prepares for Regional Elections
item.description: Over 15,000 protesters gathered in Erfurt, Germany, to block roads leading to the annual conference of the far-right Alternative for Germany (AfD) party, which re-elected its leaders Alice Weidel and ...

item.title: Oyo State Government Enhances Flood Prevention Measures Through Desilting and Public Awareness
item.description: The Oyo State Government has intensified efforts to prevent flooding by desilting waterways, conducting public awareness campaigns, and implementing erosion control projects. Commissioner Ademola Ader...

item.title: Yanga Championship Parade Faces Criticism Over Organizational Challenges
item.description: Football fans in Tanzania expressed frustration over the disorganized Yanga championship parade held on July 4, 2026, to celebrate the club's Premier League title victory. Despite the event's signific...

item.title: Chronic Kidney Disease's Impact on Patients and Families
item.description: The article discusses the profound effects of chronic kidney disease (CKD) on both patients and their loved ones, highlighting the physical, emotional, and financial burdens faced by those affected. C...

item.title: St. Anthony Mary Zaccaria: Founder of the Barnabites and His Legacy
item.description: The article highlights St. Anthony Mary Zaccaria, co-founder of the Barnabites religious congregation. Born in 1502 in Cremona, Italy, he became a medical doctor before being ordained a priest in 1528...

item.title: UK and US commemorate 250 years of independence with royal tribute and celebrations
item.description: King Charles III has praised the United States for marking the 250th anniversary of its independence from Britain, highlighting the enduring bond between the two nations. In a message to President Don...

item.title: Dish DBS Files for Bankruptcy Amid Wireless Spectrum Challenges
item.description: EchoStar's satellite pay-TV unit, Dish DBS, has filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection, according to Reuters. This move also affects its wireless subsidiaries, facilitating the wind-down of Dish W...

item.title: Kuria Advises Kenyans to Support Ruto for Second Term Amid Political Concerns
item.description: Former MP Moses Kuria has urged Kenyans to back President William Ruto for a second term, warning that removing him from office in 2027 would position him as a more formidable opposition leader by 203...

item.title: Orlando Pirates' Relebohile Mofokeng Leads PSL Awards Nominations
item.description: Relebohile Mofokeng, the star player of Orlando Pirates, has dominated the PSL awards nominations for the 2025/26 season, securing four major categories: Footballer of the Season, Players’ Player of t...

item.title: Pakistan and Turkey Commit to Strengthen Bilateral Ties and Boost Trade to $5 Billion
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif and Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan emphasized their nations' commitment to mutual support and enhancing bilateral trade to $5 billion during a joint press briefin...

item.title: Miss Behave Cast Members and Their Roles Highlighted
item.description: The article introduces the cast of the upcoming Philippine television series 'Miss Behave,' focusing on their diverse personalities and roles. It lists key actors such as Alexa Ilacad, Xyriel Manabat,...

item.title: Man arrested after Gungahlin home burglary
item.description: A 32-year-old man from Florey has been arrested and charged with burglary and theft after being caught leaving a Gungahlin home with stolen items. Police reported that the incident occurred on Friday,...

item.title: African Proverb Highlights Love and Compassion in Cultural Context
item.description: The article discusses an African proverb emphasizing love and compassion, translated into Spanish and presented in Indian English. It highlights the proverb's cultural significance, its translation pr...

item.title: Nigeria's Economic Stability Grows with Rising External Reserves
item.description: The naira showed signs of recovery in Nigeria's official foreign exchange market, appreciating by N10.74 week-on-week to N1,370.19 per dollar, marking a 0.78% gain from the previous week. This rebound...

item.title: Tanzanian Army and Government Address Peace Concerns Over Saba Saba Protests
item.description: The Tanzania People’s Defence Force (TPDF) has issued a warning against any disruption of peace amid social media calls for the Saba Saba (Seven-Seven) demonstrations on July 7, 2026. The army, throug...

item.title: World Cup 2026 Results: Argentina, Colombia, and Egypt Advance; Cape Verde, Australia, and Ghana Exit
item.description: In the FIFA World Cup 2026 Round of 32, Colombia secured a 1-0 victory over Ghana, with Argentina and Egypt also advancing to the Round of 16. Colombia dominated the match at Arrowhead Stadium but man...

item.title: Ireland edges out Wallabies in tense Nations Championship opener
item.description: In a tightly contested rugby match at Sydney Football Stadium, Ireland secured a 33-31 victory over Australia in the Nations Championship opener. The game, marked by relentless pressure and pivotal mo...
